link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部西1-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yoshimiko-jinja}} [[JA:吉御子神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部西1-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yoshimiko-jinja}} [[JA:吉御子神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946|1=811}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228|1=Ishibe-shukuba-no-sato}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980|1=Ishibe Historical Museum}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887|1=100 selected forest-bathing forests}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji|1=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji|1=Chōjū-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} * Yoshimiko-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039795|ltq=Q135039795|WD=Q135039795|1=Ishihenokashiki- Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部西1-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yoshimiko-jinja}} [[JA:吉御子神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946|1=811}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228|1=Ishibe-shukuba-no-sato}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980|1=Ishibe Historical Museum}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887|1=100 selected forest-bathing forests}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji|1=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji|1=Chōjū-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部西1-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yoshimiko-jinja}} [[JA:吉御子神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946|1=811}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228|1=Ishibe-shukuba-no-sato}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980|1=Ishibe Historical Museum}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887|1=100 selected forest-bathing forests}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji|1=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji|1=Chōjū-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} * Yoshimiko-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039795|ltq=Q135039795|WD=Q135039795|1=Ishihenokashiki- Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部西1-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yoshimiko-jinja}} [[JA:吉御子神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946|1=811}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228|1=Ishibe-shukuba-no-sato}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980|1=Ishibe Historical Museum}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887|1=100 selected forest-bathing forests}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji|1=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji|1=Chōjū-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部西1-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yoshimiko-jinja}} [[JA:吉御子神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946|1=811}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228|1=Ishibe-shukuba-no-sato}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980|1=Ishibe Historical Museum}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887|1=100 selected forest-bathing forests}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji|1=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji|1=Chōjū-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部西1-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yoshimiko-jinja}} [[JA:吉御子神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946|1=811}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228|1=Ishibe-shukuba-no-sato}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980|1=Ishibe Historical Museum}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887|1=100 selected forest-bathing forests}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji|1=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji|1=Chōjū-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} [[commons:Category:Yoshimiko-jinja]][[ja:吉御子神社]] {{Expand Japanese|吉御子神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yoshimiko Shrine | native_name = 吉御子神社 | image = Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Yoshimiko Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.010199|136.050832}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Yoshimiko Shrine'''|吉御子神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. * Yoshimiko-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039795|ltq=Q135039795|WD=Q135039795|1=Ishihenokashiki- Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部西1-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yoshimiko-jinja}} [[JA:吉御子神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946|1=811}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228|1=Ishibe-shukuba-no-sato}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980|1=Ishibe Historical Museum}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887|1=100 selected forest-bathing forests}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji|1=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji|1=Chōjū-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} [[commons:Category:Yoshimiko-jinja]][[ja:吉御子神社]] {{Expand Japanese|吉御子神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yoshimiko Shrine | native_name = 吉御子神社 | image = Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Yoshimiko Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.010199|136.050832}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Yoshimiko Shrine'''|吉御子神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部西1-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県湖南市石部東', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yoshimiko-jinja}} [[JA:吉御子神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11413129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Yoshimiko Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja02s3200.jpg|270px]]
Worship place in front of honden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=traditionally year 68 of Emperor Sujin |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshimiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshimiko-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yoshimiko Shrine''' (よしみこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, a divine descent occurred in year 68 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto and Yoshihime-no-Mikoto, children of Ukahiko, were enshrined before Tanikuro-no-Mimae; this is said to be the founding. It corresponds to the western shrine among the division of the shrine to east and west due to disaster in {{ill|lt=811|Q29946|WD=Q29946|1=811}} (Kōnin 2)[https://japan-geographic.tv/shiga/konan-yoshimikojinja.html Yoshimiko Shrine, Konan City, Shiga JAPAN-GEOGRAPHIC.TV] accessed 2013-05-01. The current honden was moved from the former honden of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kyoto in 1865 (Keiō 1), after shrine buildings were badly damaged in {{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}} (Genji 1), and was designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}} (Taishō 10). {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}} about 1 km to the south has a paired relationship as the female deity counterpart to Yoshimiko Shrine's male deity. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It has a paired relationship with {{ill|lt=Yoshihime Shrine|Q11412752|WD=Q11412752|1=Yoshihime Shrine}}, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Associated deities * Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Property (National designation) * Honden - former honden of Kamigamo Shrine in Kyoto (early Edo period architecture, moved in 1865), three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, designated April 30, 1921 * Wooden seated Yoshihiko-no-Mikoto statue (with two wooden seated attendant deity statues) - Heian period, wood ; City designated cultural property * Mikoshi === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibenishi 1-15-1 === Access === === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}. === By car === * 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ishibe-shukuba no Sato|Q11587228|WD=Q11587228|1=Ishibe-shukuba-no-sato}}, {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Ishibe-juku History and Folklore Museum|Q11527980|WD=Q11527980|1=Ishibe Historical Museum}}, Garyū no Mori ({{ill|lt=100 selected forest-bathing forests|Q11539887|WD=Q11539887|1=100 selected forest-bathing forests}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōraku-ji (Konan)|Q11481425|WD=Q11481425|lt=Jōraku-ji|1=Jōraku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Chōju-ji (Konan)|Q17214594|WD=Q17214594|lt=Chōju-ji|1=Chōjū-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Konan)|Q11579598|WD=Q11579598|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
